title: Tomas and the Time Dragon
synopsis: When ten-year-old Tomas discovers a magical dragon egg in his grandmother's attic, he's transported to medieval times where he must help a young knight named Elena and a wise scholar named Kwame save a village from a terrible curse. With the help of his new friends and a baby dragon, Tomas learns about courage, friendship, and the magic that connects all times.

chapters.title: The Mysterious Egg
chapters.content:
Tomas loved spending Saturday mornings at Grandma Rosa's house. She always had the best stories and the warmest hugs. But today, something was different.

"Mijo, I need your help," Grandma Rosa said, wiping flour from her hands on her apron. "There's an old trunk in the attic that's been making strange noises. Could you be my brave helper?"

Tomas puffed out his chest. At ten years old, he was definitely brave enough for an attic adventure. "I'll check it out, Grandma!"

The wooden stairs creaked as Tomas climbed up to the dusty attic. Sunlight streamed through a small window, making dust particles dance like tiny fairies. In the corner sat an old wooden trunk with brass corners, just like Grandma had described.

As Tomas approached, he heard it - a soft humming sound, almost like singing. He carefully lifted the heavy lid and gasped.

Inside, nestled between old quilts and photo albums, was the most beautiful egg he'd ever seen. It was about the size of a football, with swirling patterns of gold and silver that seemed to move in the light. The humming was definitely coming from the egg.

"Whoa," Tomas whispered, reaching out to touch it.

The moment his fingers made contact with the smooth shell, the world began to spin. Colors swirled around him like a rainbow tornado, and he felt like he was falling through clouds. The last thing he heard was Grandma Rosa calling his name from far, far away.

illustrations.description: A young Latino boy with dark hair and curious brown eyes kneels beside an ornate wooden trunk in a golden-lit attic. The magical egg glows with swirling gold and silver patterns, casting warm light on his amazed face. Dust motes dance in sunbeams streaming through a small window, and old quilts and family photos are visible in the trunk.
illustrations.alt: Tomas discovering the magical dragon egg in his grandmother's attic

chapters.title: A Strange New World
chapters.content:
When the spinning stopped, Tomas found himself sitting in tall grass under an enormous oak tree. The egg was still in his hands, but everything else had changed. Gone was Grandma's cozy attic. Instead, he saw rolling green hills, a dirt road, and in the distance, a castle with tall stone towers.

"Where am I?" he said aloud, looking around in wonder.

"You're in the Kingdom of Valdris," came a voice from behind him.

Tomas spun around to see a girl about his age walking toward him. She wore a simple brown tunic and had her long black hair braided back. Most surprisingly, she carried a small wooden sword at her side.

"I'm Elena," she said, eyeing the egg curiously. "And you're definitely not from around here. Those clothes are... strange."

Tomas looked down at his jeans and sneakers. She was right - they probably did look weird compared to her medieval outfit.

"I'm Tomas," he said. "I think I'm from... well, a different time. This egg brought me here somehow."

Elena's eyes widened. "A time egg! I've heard stories about those. My teacher Kwame might know more. He knows everything about magic."

Just then, the egg began to crack. Both children watched in amazement as a tiny golden snout poked through the shell, followed by two bright purple eyes.

"It's a dragon!" Elena gasped.

"A baby dragon," Tomas corrected, as the little creature - no bigger than a kitten - tumbled out of the broken shell and chirped softly.

The baby dragon looked up at Tomas and made a sound like a contented purr. Then it spoke, its voice like tinkling bells: "Tomas... friend!"

"It knows my name!" Tomas said, gently picking up the dragon. Its scales were warm and soft, like velvet.

"Dragons are very wise," Elena said seriously. "Even baby ones. Come on, we need to get to the village. Kwame will want to meet you both."

illustrations.description: A young Latino boy in modern clothes sits under a massive oak tree holding a tiny golden dragon with purple eyes. A girl with dark braided hair in medieval brown tunic stands nearby with a wooden sword. Rolling green hills and a distant stone castle create the backdrop under a warm, golden sky. Broken eggshell pieces shimmer with magical residue around them.
illustrations.alt: Tomas meeting Elena and the newly hatched baby dragon in the medieval countryside

chapters.title: The Village's Problem
chapters.content:
Elena led Tomas down the winding dirt road toward the village. The baby dragon, who had decided his name was Spark, rode contentedly on Tomas's shoulder, occasionally chirping at butterflies.

"So you're training to be a knight?" Tomas asked, noticing how Elena kept her hand on her sword hilt.

"Yep! My mom thinks I should learn to weave instead, but Sir Marcus says I have real talent with a blade. What about you? What do you want to be?"

"I don't know yet," Tomas admitted. "Maybe an inventor? Or a scientist like my dad?"

"A scientist? What's that?"

Before Tomas could explain, they reached the village. Something was wrong. The streets were nearly empty, and the few people they saw looked sad and worried.

"Where is everyone?" Elena wondered aloud.

They found their answer at the village square. A tall man with dark skin and kind eyes stood addressing a small crowd. He wore simple robes and had scrolls tucked under his arm.

"That's Kwame," Elena whispered. "He teaches all the village children and knows more about history and magic than anyone."

"...the curse has lasted three months now," Kwame was saying. "Our crops won't grow, our wells are running dry, and the animals are sick. We must find a solution soon."

An older woman with gray hair called out, "But what can we do? The Shadow Sorcerer is too powerful!"

"There has to be a way," Kwame said firmly. "We just haven't found it yet."

Elena pushed through the crowd, pulling Tomas behind her. "Master Kwame! I found someone you need to meet!"

Kwame turned and his eyes immediately focused on Spark, who had poked his tiny head out from behind Tomas's neck.

"By the ancient stars," Kwame breathed. "A time dragon hatchling. And you, young man, are definitely not from our time." His gaze moved to Tomas's modern clothes and sneakers. "Tell me, how did you come to be here?"

Tomas explained about finding the egg in his grandmother's attic. Kwame listened carefully, nodding occasionally.

"Fascinating," Kwame said when Tomas finished. "Dragons choose their companions very carefully. Perhaps you've been brought here to help us."

illustrations.description: A village square with medieval stone buildings and worried villagers gathered around a wise-looking Black man in scholar's robes. Tomas in his modern clothes stands with Elena, while tiny golden Spark peeks out from behind Tomas's shoulder. The scene has muted colors suggesting the curse's effect, but warm golden light still touches the characters' faces, showing hope.
illustrations.alt: Kwame addressing worried villagers in the cursed village square as Tomas and Elena arrive

chapters.title: Learning About the Curse
chapters.content:
Kwame invited Tomas and Elena to his study, a cozy room filled with books, maps, and strange instruments. Spark immediately flew to investigate a collection of shiny crystals on a shelf.

"Careful, little one," Kwame chuckled. "Those are very old."

"Master Kwame," Elena said, "tell Tomas about the Shadow Sorcerer."

Kwame's expression grew serious. "Lord Malachar was once a good man, but jealousy and anger turned his heart dark. He blamed our village for his troubles and cast a terrible curse upon us."

"What kind of curse?" Tomas asked.

"It drains the life from everything - plants, animals, even the water. If we don't break it soon, the village will have to be abandoned."

Spark chirped sadly and flew back to Tomas's shoulder.

"But here's the interesting part," Kwame continued. "Legend says that only a 'child from tomorrow' working with a time dragon can break such a curse."

"A child from tomorrow?" Tomas repeated. "You mean like... me?"

"Exactly like you," Kwame smiled. "Though it won't be easy. The curse can only be broken at its source - Malachar's tower."

Elena jumped up. "Then let's go! We can't waste any more time!"

"Hold on," Kwame said gently. "Malachar's magic is powerful, and his tower is protected by dark enchantments. You'll need more than courage."

He walked to his desk and picked up an old book with a glowing blue cover. "This is the Chronicle of Light Magic. It contains spells that can counter darkness, but they require three people working together."

"Three people?" Tomas asked.

"Yes. The time traveler," he pointed to Tomas, "the dragon," he nodded to Spark, "the brave heart," he smiled at Elena, "and the wise mind." He pointed to himself.

"You're coming with us?" Elena asked excitedly.

"Someone needs to make sure you don't get turned into toads," Kwame said with a wink. "Besides, I've been waiting my whole life for an adventure like this."

Tomas felt a mix of excitement and nervousness. He'd never been on a real adventure before, and certainly never had to break a magical curse. But looking at his new friends - brave Elena, wise Kwame, and little Spark who was now purring contentedly - he felt ready to try.

"When do we leave?" he asked.

"At dawn," Kwame replied. "Tonight, we prepare."

illustrations.description: A warm, candlelit study filled with ancient books, scrolls, maps, and magical instruments. Kwame, a wise Black scholar in robes, holds a glowing blue book while explaining to Tomas and Elena. Tiny golden Spark explores shiny crystals on a shelf, his purple eyes reflecting the magical light. The room has rich browns and golds with mysterious shadows in the corners.
illustrations.alt: Kwame explaining the curse and showing the Chronicle of Light Magic in his magical study

chapters.title: The Journey to the Tower
chapters.content:
The next morning, the four companions set out as the sun painted the sky pink and gold. Elena had packed supplies in a leather bag, including bread, cheese, and water. Kwame carried his magical book and a staff topped with a glowing crystal. Tomas wore a borrowed brown cloak over his modern clothes, and Spark rode proudly on his shoulder.

"The tower is a half-day's journey," Kwame explained as they walked. "We'll need to cross the Whispering Woods and climb Shadow Mountain."

"Whispering Woods?" Tomas asked nervously.

"Don't worry," Elena said cheerfully. "The trees just talk to each other. It's actually pretty cool once you get used to it."

She was right. As they entered the forest, Tomas could hear soft murmurs and whispers coming from the branches above. It wasn't scary at all - more like the trees were gossiping about the weather.

"They're talking about us," Spark announced suddenly.

"What are they saying?" Tomas asked.

Spark tilted his tiny head, listening. "They say... 'The bright ones walk toward the dark place. Hope follows their footsteps.'"

"That's beautiful," Elena said softly.

As they traveled deeper into the woods, the path became harder to follow. Thick roots crossed their way, and mysterious flowers glowed in the shadows. But Kwame seemed to know exactly where to go.

"Master Kwame," Tomas said during a rest stop, "how do you know so much about magic? I thought you were just a teacher."

Kwame smiled. "I am just a teacher. But teachers learn many things. I've studied the old stories, the ancient languages, the ways magic works in our world. Knowledge is its own kind of power."

"Like how my dad studies science," Tomas said thoughtfully. "He says understanding how things work helps you solve problems."

"Exactly," Kwame nodded. "Magic and science aren't so different. Both seek to understand the mysteries of the world."

By afternoon, they reached the base of Shadow Mountain. The cursed tower loomed above them, dark and twisted against the gray sky. Even from a distance, Tomas could feel something cold and sad about it.

"There it is," Elena said, gripping her sword handle. "Malachar's tower."

"Are you ready?" Kwame asked.

Tomas looked at his friends - Elena standing brave and strong, Kwame calm and wise, and little Spark glowing warmly on his shoulder. His grandmother always said that you're never alone when you have people who care about you.

"I'm ready," he said, and meant it.

illustrations.description: Four companions walking through a mystical forest path dappled with golden sunlight. Tall trees with glowing leaves whisper overhead while magical flowers bloom in the shadows. Tomas in his brown cloak carries tiny glowing Spark, Elena walks confidently with her sword, and Kwame leads with his crystal-topped staff. In the distance, a dark twisted tower looms on a gray mountain.
illustrations.alt: The companions journeying through the Whispering Woods toward the Shadow Sorcerer's tower

chapters.title: Facing the Shadow Sorcerer
chapters.content:
The tower was even scarier up close. Its black stone walls seemed to absorb light, and strange symbols glowed red around the entrance. But the heavy wooden door stood wide open.

"That's not a good sign," Elena whispered.

"He's expecting us," Kwame agreed grimly. "Stay close together."

Inside, the tower was cold and filled with shadows that moved on their own. Spiral stairs led upward, lit only by flickering torches. Their footsteps echoed as they climbed.

"I don't like this place," Spark whimpered, pressing closer to Tomas's neck.

"Me neither," Tomas admitted. "But we have to help the village."

At the top of the stairs, they found a large round room with windows looking out over the cursed land. In the center stood a tall man in black robes, his face pale and his eyes like empty holes.

"So," Lord Malachar said in a voice like winter wind, "the 'child from tomorrow' has come at last. And brought friends."

"Lord Malachar," Kwame stepped forward. "Your quarrel was never with the innocent people of the village. End this curse."

Malachar laughed, a sound like breaking glass. "They took everything from me! My lands, my title, my respect! Now they suffer as I have suffered."

"That's not fair!" Elena shouted, drawing her small sword. "Hurting innocent people doesn't fix your hurt!"

"Fair?" Malachar's eyes flashed red. "Nothing is fair, little knight."

He raised his hands, and dark magic swirled around them like black lightning. Tomas felt Spark tremble on his shoulder.

But then something unexpected happened. Spark began to glow brighter, and warmth spread through Tomas's chest.

"Wait," Tomas said, stepping forward. Everyone stared at him - even Malachar looked surprised.

"I know what it's like to feel left out," Tomas continued. "Sometimes at school, kids don't include me because I'm different. It hurts really bad."

Malachar's hands lowered slightly.

"But hurting other people never made me feel better," Tomas went on. "You know what did? Finding friends who cared about me." He looked at Elena and Kwame. "Real friends who like you for who you are."

Spark flew from Tomas's shoulder and hovered in front of Malachar, glowing like a tiny star. "Hurt can heal," the little dragon said softly. "Darkness can become light."

For a moment, Malachar's harsh expression softened. "But the pain... it's been so long..."

"It doesn't have to stay," Elena said gently, lowering her sword. "You could come back to the village. Help fix what's broken instead of breaking more things."

Malachar stared at them for a long moment. Then, slowly, he began to weep. As his tears fell, the red symbols on the walls flickered and went dark.

illustrations.description: Inside a dark tower room with stone walls and tall windows, a pale sorcerer in black robes faces four brave companions. Red magical symbols glow on the walls while shadows writhe unnaturally. Tiny Spark glows like a golden star between them, his light pushing back the darkness. Tomas steps forward courageously while Elena and Kwame stand supportively behind him.
illustrations.alt: The confrontation with Lord Malachar in his tower as Spark's light challenges the darkness

chapters.title: Home Again
chapters.content:
The curse broke like a soap bubble popping. Through the tower windows, they watched color return to the land. Brown fields turned green, dry streams began to flow, and flowers bloomed in fast-forward. The village bells rang joyfully in the distance.

"I... I can't believe it worked," Malachar said, staring at his hands. The dark magic was gone, replaced by a gentle silver glow.

"Friendship magic is the strongest kind," Kwame said wisely.

Lord Malachar looked at each of them. "I have much to make up for. Will the village... can they ever forgive me?"

"People are pretty good at forgiving when someone really wants to change," Elena said. "My mom always says that everyone deserves a second chance."

As they prepared to leave the tower, Spark began to glow even brighter than before.

"Time to go home, Tomas," the little dragon said sadly.

"But I don't want to leave yet!" Tomas protested. "We just became friends!"

"True friends stay friends no matter how far apart they are," Elena said, hugging him tight.

Kwame placed a hand on Tomas's shoulder. "And who knows? Perhaps the magic that brought you here will let you visit again someday."

Spark flew in a circle around all of them, leaving a trail of golden sparkles. "Remember us, Tomas. We'll remember you."

The sparkles grew brighter, and once again, Tomas felt the world spinning around him. Colors swirled like a rainbow tornado, and he had the sensation of flying through time itself.

When everything stopped spinning, he was back in Grandma Rosa's attic. The egg was gone, but in his hand was a small, smooth stone that glowed with warm golden light.

"Tomas!" Grandma's voice called from downstairs. "Did you find anything interesting up there?"

"Yeah, Grandma," he called back, carefully tucking the magic stone into his pocket. "I found some really good friends."

As he climbed down from the attic, Tomas smiled. He might be back in his own time, but he'd never forget his adventure with Elena, Kwame, and little Spark. And sometimes, when he held the magic stone, he could swear he heard the faint sound of dragon wings and the echo of friends calling his name across time.

Back in the medieval village, the harvest was the best anyone could remember, Lord Malachar had become the village's new protector, and sometimes, just sometimes, Elena and Kwame would see a flash of gold in the sky and know their friend from tomorrow was thinking of them too.

illustrations.description: A sweeping view from the tower window showing the cursed land transforming - brown fields turning emerald green, dry streams flowing with crystal water, colorful flowers blooming everywhere. Village bells ring in the distance while golden light spreads across the landscape like sunrise. The companions watch in wonder as life returns to the world.
illustrations.alt: The magical transformation of the land as the curse breaks and life returns

illustrations.description: Back in the warm, golden-lit attic, Tomas sits peacefully holding a small glowing stone. Dust motes dance in the sunbeams, and the old trunk sits nearby, now ordinary but somehow magical. His expression shows contentment and wonder as he remembers his adventure. The scene feels both cozy and touched by magic.
illustrations.alt: Tomas back in his grandmother's attic, holding the magical stone and remembering his friends

metadata.illustrationStyle: Warm, inviting adventure illustration style. Earthy tones, golden light. Detailed environments with hidden details kids can discover.

questions.question: Where does Tomas find the magical egg?
questions.explanation: Tomas discovers the magical dragon egg in his Grandma Rosa's attic while helping her investigate strange noises from an old trunk.

questions.question: What is Elena training to become?
questions.explanation: Elena is training to be a knight. She carries a wooden sword and has been learning from Sir Marcus, who says she has real talent with a blade.

questions.question: What is the baby dragon's name?
questions.explanation: The baby dragon decides his name is Spark. He's golden with purple eyes and becomes Tomas's loyal companion.

questions.question: What is causing the village's problems?
questions.explanation: Lord Malachar, the Shadow Sorcerer, has cursed the village because he feels they wronged him. The curse drains life from plants, animals, and water.

questions.question: According to legend, who can break the curse?
choices: A child from tomorrow with a time dragon
questions.explanation: The legend says only a 'child from tomorrow' working with a time dragon can break such a curse. Tomas fits this description perfectly.

questions.question: How do they ultimately break the curse?
questions.explanation: The curse is broken when Tomas shows empathy for Malachar's pain and offers friendship instead of fighting. This kindness transforms Malachar's heart and breaks the dark magic.

questions.question: What does Tomas bring back from his adventure?
questions.explanation: When Tomas returns to his own time, he has a small, smooth stone that glows with warm golden light, a reminder of his magical adventure and friendships.

pages.title: Complete the Adventure
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from Tomas's adventure!
pages.text: Tomas found a magical _____ in his grandmother's attic. When he touched it, he traveled back in _____ to meet Elena, who was training to be a _____. Together with the wise scholar _____ and baby dragon _____, they had to break Lord Malachar's terrible _____ on the village.

pages.title: Character Connections
pages.instructions: Match each character with what makes them special!
pages.text: _____ is brave and trains with a sword. _____ knows everything about magic and history. _____ is a tiny dragon who glows like gold. _____ comes from the future and has a kind heart.

pages.title: The Magic Solution
pages.instructions: Complete the story of how they solved the problem!
pages.text: Instead of fighting Lord Malachar, Tomas chose to show _____. He understood that Malachar was _____ and lonely. When Spark glowed with _____ and the friends offered _____, Malachar's heart changed and the curse was _____.
